title: Keycloak Config KOG
description: Krateo Operator Generator (KOG) that exposes Keycloak Admin API resources (realm, client, protocol-mapper, client-scope, group, identity-provider) as native Kubernetes custom resources. oasgen-provider + rest-dynamic-controller reconcile each CR against a running Keycloak Admin REST API; the admin bearer token is delivered and rotated into a Secret by the External Secrets Operator.

keycloak.title: Keycloak target
keycloak.description: Connection to the Keycloak server the generated controllers call.

baseUrl.title: Keycloak base URL
baseUrl.description: Base URL of the Keycloak server the generated controllers call (no trailing slash). Injected into every OAS asset's servers[0].url via Helm tpl. The in-cluster default points at the Service created by the operator blueprint.

auth.title: Admin bearer token
auth.description: Admin bearer token contract. rest-dynamic-controller reads a short-lived bearer token from a Secret referenced by each CR's authenticationRefs. By default the External Secrets Operator mints a fresh token from Keycloak's token endpoint (client_credentials) on a refreshInterval shorter than the token TTL and writes it into the Secret.

secretName.title: Token Secret name
secretName.description: Name of the Secret holding the Keycloak admin bearer token consumed by the generated controllers.

secretKey.title: Token Secret key
secretKey.description: Key within the Secret that holds the bearer token value.

externalSecret.title: External Secret (token rotation)
externalSecret.description: External Secrets Operator wiring that mints and rotates the Keycloak admin access token via client_credentials.

enabled.title: Enable ExternalSecret
enabled.description: When true, render the ESO Webhook generator + ExternalSecret that mint and rotate the admin bearer token. Disable to supply the token Secret yourself.

refreshInterval.title: Refresh interval
refreshInterval.description: How often ESO mints a fresh token. Keep this below the Keycloak access-token lifespan so the bearer never goes stale.

generatorRef.title: Generator reference
generatorRef.description: Reference to the ESO Generator (preferred) or SecretStore that yields the token.

apiVersion.title: Generator apiVersion
apiVersion.description: apiVersion of the ESO generator resource.

kind.title: Generator kind
kind.description: Kind of the ESO generator resource.

name.title: Generator name
name.description: Name of the ESO generator resource that mints the token.

tokenEndpoint.title: Token endpoint
tokenEndpoint.description: Keycloak OpenID Connect token endpoint the generator POSTs the client_credentials grant to.

clientId.title: Client ID
clientId.description: client_credentials client_id of the service-account client holding the required realm-management roles.

clientSecretRef.title: Client secret reference
clientSecretRef.description: Reference to a pre-existing Secret holding the service-account client's secret (kept out of values).

name.title: Secret name
name.description: Name of the Secret holding the client secret.

key.title: Secret key
key.description: Key within the Secret holding the client secret value.

restDefinitions.title: RestDefinitions
restDefinitions.description: Which RestDefinitions to emit. Each maps 1:1 to an assets/<key>.yaml OAS. The kind is intentionally prefixed Keycloak to avoid crdgen collisions with same-named lowercase body properties.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Kubernetes kind for the generated CRD.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Kubernetes kind for the generated CRD.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Kubernetes kind for the generated CRD.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Kubernetes kind for the generated CRD.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Kubernetes kind for the generated CRD.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Kubernetes kind for the generated CRD.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Kubernetes kind for the generated CRD.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Kubernetes kind for the generated CRD.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Kubernetes kind for the generated CRD.

authenticationexecution.title: Authentication execution
authenticationexecution.description: KeycloakAuthenticationExecution RestDefinition (one execution or subflow inside a flow). Lifecycle verbs are delegated to Snowplow RESTActions (observeApiRef/createApiRef/updateApiRef/deleteApiRef) because Keycloak's executions API is create-then-mutate with move-op ordering. Requires the snowplow endpoint Secret (snowplow.endpointSecretName).

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Emit the KeycloakAuthenticationExecution RestDefinition plus its backing RESTActions and endpoint Secret.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Kubernetes kind for the generated CRD.

snowplow.title: Snowplow delegation
snowplow.description: Settings for the RESTAction-delegated resources (currently only KeycloakAuthenticationExecution).

endpointSecretName.title: Endpoint Secret name
endpointSecretName.description: Name of the snowplow Endpoint-shaped Secret (keys: server-url, token) the authentication-execution RESTActions call Keycloak through. Rendered in the release namespace from the same rotated admin token when auth.externalSecret.enabled is true; otherwise supply it yourself.

resourceGroup.title: Resource group
resourceGroup.description: Kubernetes API group the generated CRDs register under. GVK = <group>/v1alpha1.

verbose.title: Verbose logging
verbose.description: Enable verbose logging on the generated connectors (annotation krateo.io/connector-verbose).
